title: Prototypes, failures, and unexpected breakthroughs
key_points: Noah Finer describes two contrasting engineering mindsets at Figma: building quick, 'janky' prototypes for interactions versus perfecting subtle editor microinteractions that are designed to go almost unnoticed.
The talk explores how these two approaches interact and inform each other, framing prototyping, failures, and unexpected breakthroughs as part of the building process.
Noah draws on concrete experiences shipping features like Figma Slides, FigPals, and interactive transit personality tests to illustrate how these mindsets shape the experiences he builds.
